column5:
The opening of our new StoneRise Technology head office in London this January marks an important milestone for the business and the next stage of our development.

This move is driven by one purpose: to create the right environment for a high performing technology team to scale. Bringing everyone together in a dedicated space strengthens alignment, accelerates decision making and ensures we maintain the pace required as we move toward our first product launch.

Being located in London also allows us to deepen our relationships with partners, clients and collaborators. It gives us closer access to the talent, expertise and networks that will support StoneRise Technology as we continue to grow. Creating a home for the business is about more than desks and meeting rooms. It is about building the foundation for long term success.

We have ambitious plans for 2025 and beyond, and this new office is a clear step forward in achieving them.

Learn more about StoneRise:

#BuiltByConstruction4Construction #StoneRise #ChallengingTheStatusQuo

Proud to be sharing this milestone moment for Mike and for Milestone Group.

I have had the privilege of working closely with Mike for many years and have seen first-hand the commitment, ambition and sheer determination he has poured into building Milestone into the business it is today. The journey has had incredible highs and some very real challenges.

Many of us will remember the photo of Mike in ICU on a ventilator with his laptop still open, trying to keep things moving... 6 days later he dragged himself into the office to Chair an SMT meeting before crawling home to bed. There are not many people on earth as relentlessly committed as he is.

This next chapter feels absolutely right for Mike. His focus on StoneRise and The Loizias Foundation comes from a place of purpose and a drive to create meaningful change for our industry and for those living with auto immune conditions.

While his role at Milestone is shifting, we will very much continue working together at board level to support the long-term success of the business he started and we've built. And at StoneRise, I am looking forward to working side by side with him as we take the company to even greater heights.

Before StoneRise Technology, Michael Loizias and I ran a construction business turning over £30m+.

I spent years as Finance and Commercial Director. I know exactly where the hidden costs sit in a contracting operation. The admin hours that never get measured. The errors that creep in through spreadsheets. The cash tied up because invoices are not being chased efficiently.

Here’s the reality. Most contractors are losing 2 to 3 percent of margin to inefficient processes. Not materials. Not labour. Just admin and avoidable errors.

On a £10m turnover, that is £200k to £300k every year. Money that adds no value and goes nowhere.

That is why we built StoneRise. Not because construction needs more software, but because contractors need to protect their margins.

If you want to understand where your hidden costs sit, I am always happy to talk.

Curious where your hidden costs sit? Run your numbers here: https://www.stonerise.tech/products/procurement/ROI?utm_source=linkedin_stelios&utm_medium=organic_social&utm_campaign=roi_calculator_general&utm_content=20260202_post1

#BuiltByConstruction4Construction #StoneRise #ChallengingTheStatusQuo

Michael’s post yesterday highlighted how much time is wasted in construction simply trying to keep people compliant. From a business perspective, the impact goes much further than admin frustration.

Poor training management creates real cost. Projects slow down when cards expire, onboarding drags, audits become stressful, and companies end up carrying far more risk than they realise. Most importantly, managers spend hours chasing information instead of leading their teams.

What we are doing with the StoneRise HR module is bringing structure and certainty to an area that has held businesses back for years. One view of compliance across the company. Clear renewal alerts. No gaps, no surprises, no excuses. The efficiency gain alone is significant, but the reduction in operational and commercial risk is even more valuable.

This is not just a nicer way of managing HR. It is a measurable improvement to how a construction business runs.

If you want a cleaner, safer and more organised way of managing your workforce, StoneRise is built for exactly that.

We’re looking for a few more early adopters to work with us on the Procurement module within the StoneRise Technology ERP platform.

This is the first live release in a wider system we’re building to remove friction from how construction businesses manage commercial and financial processes.

For a small number of contractors, we’re offering:
6 months of unlimited free access
The opportunity to influence how the platform develops
Direct access to the founding team
Early visibility of upcoming modules

This isn’t about selling software. It’s about working closely with a handful of forward-thinking businesses to ensure we’re solving real operational problems before scaling.

If you’re running procurement today and feel current systems don’t give you the clarity or control you need, feel free to reach out or comment below.

Michael's right. Invoice AI is one of those features where you wonder why it took so long for someone to build it properly.

We've been using it at Milestone for months now. Hundreds of invoices processed. The time saving is obvious. But the real value is in what it catches.

Duplicate invoices. Incorrect quantities. Price discrepancies. The kind of errors that slip through when someone's manually checking 50 invoices in a day.

The system flags them automatically. Your team reviews the exceptions. Everything else flows straight through.

On a £20m turnover, even catching 0.5% of invoice errors is £100k protected. That's not software cost. That's bottom line.

Worth seeing if you're serious about tightening up procurement controls.
